attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CIA (Common Infections & Afflictions) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Operator ruled (option A) that the paediatric weight-based dosing sealed in CIA-032 (croup) is acceptable as clinician-administered answer-key content.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CIA casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Age inferred as ~42 and sex inferred as male from 82 kg body weight, 'living alone' and 'independent' with no age or sex stated in the source note — please confirm on clinician review.
Geography inferred as RA1_major_city (private residence with an after-hours home visit available) — flagged.

Tier calibration: source note's 'urgency justification' over-triages a benign self-limiting cold to an urgent after-hours on-site assessment. Reprojected to the true low acuity (T1, self-care + safety-net); emergency escalation of the presentation itself is scored as over-triage, not caution.

Telehealth reprojection applied: clinician anterior rhinoscopy, measured vitals and clear-lung auscultation are sealed in nodes 10/11 as cannot_be_elicited_via_telehealth; patient/video-observable data only in 01 objective_data_offered (verified:false).
Provisional case_id SPEC-RESP-01-00205 — ingest --reseq will assign the real sequence.
Candidate codes only (SNOMED CT-AU / ICD-10-AM / AMT) registered unverified_pending_terminology_receipt; no dose synthesised beyond the source note; no paediatric dosing (adult case).

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
